perturbation.original_text: Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to the agreement may authorize each other party to file on its behalf amendments to such statement, provided prior written consent is obtained from the party whose information is being amended.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'expressly authorizes' with 'may authorize, provided prior written consent is obtained'. The original contract clearly stated that each party authorized each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement, whereas the modified version introduces a condition of obtaining prior written consent. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is not clear if the authorization is automatic (original text) or conditional (modified text).
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13G (including amendments thereto) with respect to the Common Stock of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further agree that this agreement be included as an exhibit to such filing. Each party to the agreement may authorize each other party to file on its behalf amendments to such statement, provided prior written consent is obtained from the party whose information is being amended. Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly stated that each party authorized each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement, whereas the modified version introduces a condition of obtaining prior written consent. This directly contradicts the initial understanding of authorization by adding a condition. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, this contradiction makes it unclear if the authorization is automatic (original text) or conditional (modified text) . Thus, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In evidence whereof, the undersigned have caused this Agreement to be executed on their behalf this 7t h day of April, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: In evidence whereof, the undersigned have attempted to execute this Agreement on their behalf this 7t h day of April, 2020, subject to ratification by a majority vote of shareholders.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that the agreement has been executed, implying finality. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the agreement has only been 'attempted to execute' and is 'subject to ratification by a majority vote of shareholders'. This contradiction creates uncertainty as to whether the signatures are legally binding before shareholder approval. If they haven't executed it, what does that mean for this?
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13G (including amendments thereto) with respect to the Common Stock of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further agree that this agreement be included as an exhibit to such filing. Each party to the agreement may authorize each other party to file on its behalf amendments to such statement, provided prior written consent is obtained from the party whose information is being amended. Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts execution, while the changed text introduces the condition of shareholder ratification, thereby creating uncertainty about the agreement's finality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a fully executed agreement for joint filing under the Securities Exchange Act. The changed text introduces ambiguity (an Ambiguity In-Text Contradiction) that would likely cause confusion as to whether the agreement is legally binding and enforceable before shareholder approval. If it is not fully 'executed', what is the basis for the agreement?

perturbation.original_text: Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to this agreement believes that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, although enforceability is not guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty and a potential contradiction regarding the enforceability of the agreement. The original statement 'agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts' suggests a firm understanding of the legality and acceptance of counterpart signatures. The changed statement introduces doubt with 'believes that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, although enforceability is not guaranteed'. This directly contradicts the earlier statement because it casts uncertainty on the legal validity and enforceability of using counterpart signatures, which can be problematic if one party disputes the validity based on this altered clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13G (including amendments thereto) with respect to the Common Stock of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further agree that this agreement be included as an exhibit to such filing. Each party to the agreement may authorize each other party to file on its behalf amendments to such statement, provided prior written consent is obtained from the party whose information is being amended. Each party to this agreement believes that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, although enforceability is not guaranteed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty regarding the enforceability of counterpart signatures, directly contradicting the original agreement's implied certainty. The original text states that 'Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.' The changed text states that parties 'believes that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, although enforceability is not guaranteed.' This introduces a clear contradiction because the agreement to use counterpart signatures is now qualified with doubt about its legal validity, undermining the enforceability of the joint filing agreement if counterpart signatures are used and subsequently challenged. This corresponds to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
